heading: 9.7.14.6.2.6.Matrix Fragments for sparsemma.m16n8k64with.u8/.s8/.e4m3/.e5m2type
content:
A warp executing sparse `mma.m16n8k64` with `.u8` / `.s8`/ `.e4m3`/ `.e5m2` /
`.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` type will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k64`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.u8` / `.s8` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing four non-zero `.u8` / `.s8` elements out of 8 consecutive elements from matrix A. | Mapping of the non-zero elements is as described in [Sparse matrix storage](#warp-level-sparse-matrix-storage). |
  | `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing four non-zero `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` elements out of 8 consecutive elements from matrix A. |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 130](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-a-first32col)
  and [Figure 131](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-a-last32col).

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-A-first32col]
SPARSE MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
----------------------------------
Instruction: MMA .m16n8k64
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=64
This figure covers columns 0-31 of matrix A (the first half of the K=64 dimension)
Data types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1

2. GRID STRUCTURE
-----------------
Rows (matrix rows): 0 through 15 (M=16 rows total)
Columns (matrix columns): 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31 (four groups of 8 columns each)
Threads: T0 through T31 (32 threads in a warp)
Register elements per thread shown: a0, a1, a2, a3 (rows 0-7) and a4, a5, a6, a7 (rows 8-15)

3. COMPLETE THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING
--------------------------------------

UPPER HALF (Matrix rows 0-7, Register elements a0, a1, a2, a3):

Row 0:
  Columns 0-7:   T0:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 8-15:  T1:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 16-23: T2:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 24-31: T3:{a0,a1,a2,a3}

Row 1:
  Columns 0-7:   T4:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 8-15:  T5:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 16-23: T6:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 24-31: T7:{a0,a1,a2,a3}

Row 2: (pattern continues with T8-T11, implied by arrows)
Row 3: (T12-T15)
Row 4: (T16-T19)
Row 5: (T20-T23)
Row 6: (T24-T27)

Row 7:
  Columns 0-7:   T28:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 8-15:  T29:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 16-23: T30:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  Columns 24-31: T31:{a0,a1,a2,a3}

LOWER HALF (Matrix rows 8-15, Register elements a4, a5, a6, a7):

Row 8:
  Columns 0-7:   T0:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 8-15:  T1:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 16-23: T2:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 24-31: T3:{a4,a5,a6,a7}

Row 9:
  Columns 0-7:   T4:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 8-15:  T5:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 16-23: T6:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 24-31: T7:{a4,a5,a6,a7}

Row 10: (pattern continues with T8-T11, implied by arrows)
Row 11: (T12-T15)
Row 12: (T16-T19)
Row 13: (T20-T23)
Row 14: (T24-T27)

Row 15:
  Columns 0-7:   T28:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 8-15:  T29:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 16-23: T30:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  Columns 24-31: T31:{a4,a5,a6,a7}

4. GROUPING PATTERNS
---------------------
Thread assignment pattern: Each matrix row is covered by 4 threads (one per 8-column group).
Rows 0-7 map to threads T0-T31 in groups of 4 per row:
  Row 0 -> T0, T1, T2, T3
  Row 1 -> T4, T5, T6, T7
  Row 2 -> T8, T9, T10, T11
  Row 3 -> T12, T13, T14, T15
  Row 4 -> T16, T17, T18, T19
  Row 5 -> T20, T21, T22, T23
  Row 6 -> T24, T25, T26, T27
  Row 7 -> T28, T29, T30, T31

Rows 8-15 reuse the SAME threads T0-T31 in the same grouping pattern, but use register elements a4-a7 instead of a0-a3.

5. REGISTER PACKING
--------------------
Each thread holds 8 register elements total for this fragment: a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7.
Elements a0-a3 cover matrix rows 0-7 (columns 0-31).
Elements a4-a7 cover matrix rows 8-15 (columns 0-31).
For 8-bit types (u8/s8) and floating-point 8-bit types (e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1): four 8-bit values are packed into each 32-bit register, so {a0,a1,a2,a3} occupies one 32-bit register and {a4,a5,a6,a7} occupies one 32-bit register, giving 2 registers per thread total for this fragment covering columns 0-31.
Each thread covers 8 consecutive columns within its assigned column group (spanning 8 columns out of the 32 shown).


  Figure 130 Sparse MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for columns 0–31 of matrix A with `.u8`/`.s8`/`.e4m3`/`.e5m2`/`.e3m2`/`.e2m3`/`.e2m1` type.

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-A-last32col]
SPARSE MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
Figure: Sparse MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for columns 32-63 of matrix A
Data types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=64
This figure covers columns 32 through 63 (the second half of the K dimension)

GRID STRUCTURE
Rows represent matrix rows (0 through 15)
Columns represent matrix column ranges (32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63)
Each cell contains a thread identifier and the register elements that thread holds for that row/column range

FULL LAYOUT TABLE

Row\Column | 32...39              | 40...47              | 48...55              | 56...63
-----------|----------------------|----------------------|----------------------|----------------------
0          | T0:{a8,a9,a10,a11}   | T1:{a8,a9,a10,a11}   | T2:{a8,a9,a10,a11}   | T3:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
1          | T4:{a8,a9,a10,a11}   | T5:{a8,a9,a10,a11}   | T6:{a8,a9,a10,a11}   | T7:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
2          | (implied pattern)    | (implied pattern)    | (implied pattern)    | (implied pattern)
...        | ...                  | ...                  | ...                  | ...
7          | T28:{a8,a9,a10,a11}  | T29:{a8,a9,a10,a11}  | T30:{a8,a9,a10,a11}  | T31:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
8          | T0:{a12,a13,a14,a15} | T1:{a12,a13,a14,a15} | T2:{a12,a13,a14,a15} | T3:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
9          | T4:{a12,a13,a14,a15} | T5:{a12,a13,a14,a15} | T6:{a12,a13,a14,a15} | T7:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
10         | (implied pattern)    | (implied pattern)    | (implied pattern)    | (implied pattern)
...        | ...                  | ...                  | ...                  | ...
15         | T28:{a12,a13,a14,a15}| T29:{a12,a13,a14,a15}| T30:{a12,a13,a14,a15}| T31:{a12,a13,a14,a15}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERNS

Matrix rows 0-7:
  Threads T0-T3 cover row 0, columns 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
  Threads T4-T7 cover row 1, columns 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
  Pattern continues with groups of 4 threads per row
  Threads T28-T31 cover row 7, columns 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
  All threads in rows 0-7 hold register elements a8, a9, a10, a11

Matrix rows 8-15:
  Threads T0-T3 cover row 8, columns 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
  Threads T4-T7 cover row 9, columns 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
  Pattern continues with groups of 4 threads per row
  Threads T28-T31 cover row 15, columns 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
  All threads in rows 8-15 hold register elements a12, a13, a14, a15

REGISTER ELEMENT DETAILS

For matrix rows 0-7 (columns 32-63):
  Each thread holds 4 register elements: a8, a9, a10, a11
  These are packed into registers (4 elements per thread for this column range)

For matrix rows 8-15 (columns 32-63):
  Each thread holds 4 register elements: a12, a13, a14, a15
  These are packed into registers (4 elements per thread for this column range)

PACKING NOTE
For 8-bit types (u8/s8) and sub-byte float types (e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1), multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register. Four element labels (e.g., a8,a9,a10,a11) per thread per column group indicates 4 values packed into the register(s) assigned to that thread for that portion of the matrix. The diagonal arrows in the figure indicate the thread-to-row mapping continues in the same repeating pattern for rows 2-6 and rows 10-14 (not explicitly shown but implied by the arrows connecting the visible rows).


  Figure 131 Sparse MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for columns 32–63 of matrix A with `.u8`/`.s8`/`.e4m3`/`.e5m2`/`.e3m2`/`.e2m3`/`.e2m1` type.

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID            for ai where  0 <= i < 4 || 8 <= i < 12
             groupID + 8        Otherwise

  col = [firstcol ... lastcol]  // As per the mapping of non-zero elements
                                // as described in Sparse matrix storage

  Where
  firstcol = threadID_in_group * 8           For ai where i <  8
             (threadID_in_group * 8) + 32    For ai where i >= 8
  lastcol  = firstcol + 7
  ```
* Multiplicand B:

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.u8` / `.s8` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, each containing four `.u8` / `.s8` elements from matrix B. | b0, b1, b2, b3, …, b15 |
  | `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, each containing four `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` / `.e3m2` / `.e2m3` / `.e2m1` elements from matrix B. |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 132](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-b1),
  [Figure 133](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-b2), [Figure 134](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-b3) and [Figure 135](#sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-b4).

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-B1]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k64 MATRIX B FRAGMENT LAYOUT
Data Types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1
Matrix: B (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=64

GRID STRUCTURE OVERVIEW:
- Rows (matrix rows covered): 0-15, divided into 4 bands of 4 rows each
- Columns (register groups): 0, 1, 2, ..., 7 (8 column groups total)
- Each thread holds 4 register elements: b0, b1, b2, b3
- Thread indexing label at bottom: %laneid:{fragments}

DETAILED THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING:

Row band 0 (matrix rows 0-3):
  Column 0: T0  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 1: T4  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 2: (implied, shown with arrow continuation)
  ...
  Column 7: T28 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row band 1 (matrix rows 4-7):
  Column 0: T1  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 1: T5  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T29 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row band 2 (matrix rows 8-11):
  Column 0: T2  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 1: T6  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T30 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

Row band 3 (matrix rows 12-15):
  Column 0: T3  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 1: T7  holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}
  Column 2: (implied)
  ...
  Column 7: T31 holds {b0, b1, b2, b3}

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0-T3 cover column 0, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
- Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
- Threads T8-T11 cover column 2 (implied), same row pattern
- Pattern continues: threads T(4*col + row_band) cover column col, row band row_band
- Threads T28-T31 cover column 7, rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively
- Total threads used: 32 (T0 through T31)
- Total columns: 8 (columns 0 through 7)
- 4 threads per column group, 8 column groups = 32 threads total

REGISTER PACKING:
- Each thread holds 4 register elements labeled b0, b1, b2, b3
- These are packed into 32-bit registers
- For 8-bit types (.u8/.s8/.e4m3/.e5m2/.e3m2/.e2m3/.e2m1): 4 elements packed per 32-bit register (b0, b1, b2, b3 packed into one 32-bit register word)
- Each thread thus uses 1 register of 32 bits containing 4 packed 8-bit (or sub-8-bit) values

SUMMARY:
- 32 threads (T0-T31) each hold 4 elements (b0-b3) packed in registers
- Thread Tn covers: row band = (n mod 4), column group = floor(n / 4)
- Column groups span columns 0-7 of the B matrix fragment
- Row bands span rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 of the B matrix fragment


  Figure 132 Sparse MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for rows 0–15 of matrix B with `.u8`/`.s8`/`.e4m3`/`.e5m2`/`.e3m2`/`.e2m3`/`.e2m1` type.

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-B2]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k64 MATRIX B FRAGMENT LAYOUT - ROWS 16-31

OVERVIEW
Matrix: B (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=64 (sparse)
This figure covers rows 16-31 of matrix B
Data types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1
Thread indexing: %laneid:{fragments}

GRID STRUCTURE
Rows (vertical axis): Matrix rows 16-31, grouped into sets of 4
Columns (horizontal axis): Register columns 0, 1, 2, ..., 7 (8 columns total, with columns 3-6 implied by "...")

REGISTER ELEMENT NAMING
Each thread holds 4 register elements named b4, b5, b6, b7 (a vector/struct of 4 values packed together)

THREAD-TO-ROW MAPPING

Row group 16-19 (rows 16, 17, 18, 19):
  Column 0: T0  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Column 1: T4  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Column 2: (implied, part of "..." range)
  Columns 3-6: (implied by "...")
  Column 7: T28 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row group 20-23 (rows 20, 21, 22, 23):
  Column 0: T1  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Column 1: T5  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Column 2: (implied)
  Columns 3-6: (implied by "...")
  Column 7: T29 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row group 24-27 (rows 24, 25, 26, 27):
  Column 0: T2  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Column 1: T6  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Column 2: (implied)
  Columns 3-6: (implied by "...")
  Column 7: T30 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

Row group 28-31 (rows 28, 29, 30, 31):
  Column 0: T3  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Column 1: T7  holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}
  Column 2: (implied)
  Columns 3-6: (implied by "...")
  Column 7: T31 holds {b4, b5, b6, b7}

COMPLETE THREAD ASSIGNMENT TABLE

Thread | Matrix Rows | Column | Registers
T0     | 16-19       | 0      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T1     | 20-23       | 0      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T2     | 24-27       | 0      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T3     | 28-31       | 0      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T4     | 16-19       | 1      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T5     | 20-23       | 1      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T6     | 24-27       | 1      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T7     | 28-31       | 1      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T8-T27 | 16-31       | 2-6    | b4, b5, b6, b7 (implied by "..." in both row and column directions)
T28    | 16-19       | 7      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T29    | 20-23       | 7      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T30    | 24-27       | 7      | b4, b5, b6, b7
T31    | 28-31       | 7      | b4, b5, b6, b7

PACKING DETAILS
Each thread holds 4 register elements: b4, b5, b6, b7
These are packed into 32-bit registers. For 8-bit types (u8/s8/e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1), four 8-bit values fit into one 32-bit register, so b4, b5, b6, b7 together occupy 2 registers (two 8-bit values packed per register, or all four in two 32-bit registers depending on packing).
The elements b4-b7 represent the register index offset from the base register for this fragment (continuing from b0-b3 used in rows 0-15).

PATTERN SUMMARY
- 4 row groups of 4 rows each span rows 16-31
- 8 column groups (columns 0-7) correspond to threads T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31
- Within each row-group/column intersection, one thread holds 4 elements (b4, b5, b6, b7)
- Thread numbering: T(row_group + 4*col_group) where row_group in {0,1,2,3} and col_group in {0,1,...,7}
- This is the continuation (rows 16-31) of the full 32-row B matrix layout


  Figure 133 Sparse MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for rows 16–31 of matrix B with `.u8`/`.s8`/`.e4m3`/`.e5m2`/`.e3m2`/`.e2m3`/`.e2m1` type.

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-B3]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k64 MATRIX B FRAGMENT LAYOUT - ROWS 32-47

=== OVERVIEW ===
Matrix: B (input matrix)
Operation dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=64
This figure covers rows 32-47 of matrix B (the second half of the B matrix row range)
Data types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1
Thread indexing: %laneid:{fragments}
Columns shown: 0, 1, 2, ..., 7 (8 columns total)

=== GRID STRUCTURE ===
Rows (matrix rows 32-47) are divided into 4 groups of 4 rows each:
  Row group 1: rows 32-35
  Row group 2: rows 36-39
  Row group 3: rows 40-43
  Row group 4: rows 44-47

Columns: 0 through 7 (8 columns)

=== THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING ===

Each thread holds 4 register elements: b8, b9, b10, b11 (packed in registers)

ROW GROUP: rows 32-35
  Column 0: T0  holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}
  Column 1: T4  holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}
  Column 2: (continues pattern, arrow indicates progression)
  ...
  Column 7: T28 holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}

ROW GROUP: rows 36-39
  Column 0: T1  holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}
  Column 1: T5  holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}
  Column 2: (continues pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: T29 holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}

ROW GROUP: rows 40-43
  Column 0: T2  holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}
  Column 1: T6  holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}
  Column 2: (continues pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: T30 holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}

ROW GROUP: rows 44-47
  Column 0: T3  holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}
  Column 1: T7  holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}
  Column 2: (continues pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: T31 holds {b8, b9, b10, b11}

=== THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN ===
Thread ID within each column group follows this pattern:
  Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3  (for row groups 32-35, 36-39, 40-43, 44-47 respectively)
  Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
  Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
  Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
  Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
  Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
  Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
  Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

General formula: Thread = (column_index * 4) + row_group_index
  where row_group_index: 0=rows32-35, 1=rows36-39, 2=rows40-43, 3=rows44-47

=== REGISTER ELEMENT DETAILS ===
Each thread stores 4 elements labeled b8, b9, b10, b11
These are the SECOND set of 4 register elements (b0-b7 appear in rows 0-31, b8-b11 appear in rows 32-47)
For 8-bit data types (u8/s8) or floating point types (e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1):
  Multiple 8-bit elements are packed into each 32-bit register
  b8 and b9 are packed into one 32-bit register
  b10 and b11 are packed into one 32-bit register
  (4 elements total per thread = 2 registers of 32 bits each, with 2 elements per register for 8-bit types)

=== TOTAL THREAD COVERAGE ===
All 32 threads (T0-T31) are used
Each thread covers one specific (row-group, column) intersection
32 threads x 4 elements = 128 element slots covering the 16-row x 8-column sparse B fragment for rows 32-47


  Figure 134 Sparse MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for rows 32–47 of matrix B with `.u8`/`.s8`/`.e4m3`/`.e5m2`/`.e3m2`/`.e2m3`/`.e2m1` type.

  

[Figure: sparse-mma-16864-u8s8-B4]
SPARSE MMA .m16n8k64 Fragment Layout - Matrix B, Rows 48-63
Data Types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1

=== MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND CONTEXT ===
Operation: MMA .m16n8k64
Matrix: B (input matrix)
This figure covers rows 48 through 63 of matrix B
Fragment identifier: %laneid:{fragments}

=== GRID STRUCTURE ===
Rows (vertical axis): Matrix rows 48-63, grouped into 4 bands of 4 rows each
  Band 1: rows 48-51
  Band 2: rows 52-55
  Band 3: rows 56-59
  Band 4: rows 60-63

Columns (horizontal axis): Column indices 0, 1, 2, ..., 7 (8 columns total, with "..." indicating columns 3-6 exist but are not individually labeled)

=== THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING ===

Each thread holds 4 register elements labeled b12, b13, b14, b15 (packed as a group/fragment).

COLUMN 0:
  Thread T0:  rows 48-51,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T1:  rows 52-55,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T2:  rows 56-59,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T3:  rows 60-63,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}

COLUMN 1:
  Thread T4:  rows 48-51,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T5:  rows 52-55,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T6:  rows 56-59,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T7:  rows 60-63,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}

COLUMN 2:
  (Threads implied by pattern, shown with arrow indicating continuation)
  Threads T8-T11 cover rows 48-63 respectively (following same pattern)

COLUMNS 3-6: (indicated by "..." in column header)
  Threads T12-T27 cover rows 48-63 following the same pattern

COLUMN 7:
  Thread T28: rows 48-51,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T29: rows 52-55,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T30: rows 56-59,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}
  Thread T31: rows 60-63,  registers {b12, b13, b14, b15}

=== GROUPING PATTERN ===
- 32 threads total (T0 through T31) are used
- Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per column
- Each group of 4 threads (e.g., T0-T3) covers all 16 rows (48-63) within one column
- Each thread covers exactly 4 consecutive rows within its assigned column
- 8 columns total, each column assigned to 4 threads:
    Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
    Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
    Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
    Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
    Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
    Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
    Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
    Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

=== REGISTER PACKING ===
- Each thread holds 4 register elements: b12, b13, b14, b15
- These are the 13th through 16th register fragments (0-indexed: b12=index 12, b13=index 13, b14=index 14, b15=index 15)
- For 8-bit types (.u8/.s8) and floating point sub-byte types (.e4m3/.e5m2/.e3m2/.e2m3/.e2m1): multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register
- The 4 elements {b12, b13, b14, b15} represent the fragment registers assigned to this row range (rows 48-63); earlier row ranges (rows 0-15, 16-31, 32-47) use register sets b0-b3, b4-b7, b8-b11 respectively
- The notation %laneid:{fragments} indicates the fragment assignment is determined by the lane (thread) ID within the warp


  Figure 135 Sparse MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for rows 48–63 of matrix B with `.u8`/`.s8`/`.e4m3`/`.e5m2`/`.e3m2`/`.e2m3`/`.e2m1` type.
* Matrix fragments for accumulators C and D are the same as in case of
  [Matrix Fragments for mma.m16n8k16 with integer type](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16816-i8-f8).
* Metadata: A `.b32` register containing 16 2-bit vectors with each pair of 2-bit vectors storing
  the indices of two non-zero elements from a 4-wide chunk of matrix A as shown in
  [Figure 136](#sparse-mma-metadata-16864-u8s8-first32col) and [Figure 137](#sparse-mma-metadata-16864-u8s8-last32col).

The table has rows labeled 0–15 (matrix rows) and columns labeled by 4-wide column groups: 0..3, 4..7, 8..11, 12..15, 16..19, 20..23, 24..27, 28..31.

Each cell contains: Thread identifier (T_n) and bit range within that thread's metadata register, in the format T_n: [high..low].

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN

Rows 0–7 use threads T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28 (stride of +4 per row).
  Row 0 -> T0
  Row 1 -> T4
  Row 2 -> T8
  Row 3 -> T12
  Row 4 -> T16
  Row 5 -> T20
  Row 6 -> T24
  Row 7 -> T28

Rows 8–15 use threads T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29 (stride of +4 per row, offset +1 from upper half).
  Row 8  -> T1
  Row 9  -> T5
  Row 10 -> T9
  Row 11 -> T13
  Row 12 -> T17
  Row 13 -> T21
  Row 14 -> T25
  Row 15 -> T29

BIT FIELD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN

Each thread holds a single 32-bit metadata register covering all 8 column groups (cols 0..31).
Each group of 4 columns consumes exactly 4 bits of the metadata register.
The bit fields are contiguous and non-overlapping across the 32-bit register:

  Cols 0..3:   bits [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   bits [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  bits [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: bits [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: bits [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: bits [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: bits [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: bits [31..28]

This means each 4-bit field encodes the sparsity selector for 4 consecutive columns within one matrix row. For 2:4 structured sparsity, each group of 4 elements contains exactly 2 non-zeros; 4 bits encode two 2-bit selectors (each 2-bit value selects which of the 4 positions is non-zero).

DIAGONAL ARROWS
Two diagonal arrows are shown in the figure:
1. An arrow from the row 1 / cols 28..31 cell (T4: [31..28]) pointing diagonally down-left toward row 7 / cols 0..3 (T28: [3..0]), indicating the continuation of the thread sequence T4 -> T8 -> ... -> T28 across rows 2–7.
2. An arrow from the row 9 / cols 28..31 cell (T5: [31..28]) pointing diagonally down-left toward row 15 / cols 0..3 (T29: [3..0]), indicating the continuation of the thread sequence T5 -> T9 -> ... -> T29 across rows 10–15.

SUMMARY OF KEY STRUCTURAL RULES
1. Each matrix row maps to exactly one thread.
2. Each thread's 32-bit register covers all 32 columns (cols 0..31) for that row.
3. Bit fields are 4 bits wide per 4-column group, packed LSB to MSB left to right.
4. Upper 8 rows (0–7) use even-indexed threads in multiples of 4: T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28.
5. Lower 8 rows (8–15) use odd-indexed threads offset by 1: T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29.
6. Threads T2, T3, T6, T7, T10, T11, etc. (those with index mod 4 = 2 or 3) are not used for this metadata layout in columns 0–31 (they likely cover columns 32–63, the second half of k=64).

THREAD PROGRESSION PATTERN:
Rows 0-7 use threads: T2, T6, ..., T30 (even-numbered threads starting at 2, incrementing by 4: T2, T6, T10, T14, T18, T22, T26, T30)
Rows 8-15 use threads: T3, T7, ..., T31 (odd-numbered threads starting at 3, incrementing by 4: T3, T7, T11, T15, T19, T23, T27, T31)

BIT ALLOCATION PER THREAD:
Each thread holds a 32-bit metadata register [31..0].
The 32 bits are divided into 8 groups of 4 bits each:
  Bits [3..0]   -> column group 35..32
  Bits [7..4]   -> column group 39..36
  Bits [11..8]  -> column group 43..40
  Bits [15..12] -> column group 47..44
  Bits [19..16] -> column group 51..48
  Bits [23..20] -> column group 55..52
  Bits [27..24] -> column group 59..56
  Bits [31..28] -> column group 63..60

ENCODING FORMAT:
Each 4-bit field encodes sparsity metadata for 4 consecutive columns. In 2:4 structured sparsity, 2 non-zero elements are selected out of every 4 elements. Each 4-bit group encodes which 2 of the 4 elements in that column group are non-zero (2 bits per selected element index).

DIAGONAL ARROWS:
Two diagonal arrows are visible in the figure, indicating the reading/continuation direction of the thread pattern across rows 2-7 (upper arrow) and rows 10-15 (lower arrow), showing that intermediate rows follow the same incremental thread numbering pattern between the explicitly shown boundary rows.

RELATIONSHIP TO COLUMNS 0-31:
This figure covers columns 32-63 only. The companion figure for columns 0-31 covers the first half of the k=64 dimension. Threads T2/T6/.../T30 and T3/T7/.../T31 each hold the full 32-bit metadata for both halves of their assigned rows, with bits [31..0] covering columns 32-63 as shown here.
